case.author: Walter Isaacson
case.chapter_title: Chapter Thirty-Four: Twenty-first-century Macs: Setting Apple Apart
case.selection_reason: Excerpt captures Jobs criticizing a design for lacking 'purity' and advocating that 'each element be true to itself' - exemplifies his perfectionist approach to product design and aesthetic philosophy.
case.judge_focus: Evaluate whether the model can identify Jobs' design philosophy emphasizing purity and holistic integrity in product engineering.
case.excerpt_text:
Jobs didn’t like it.
As he often did, both at Pixar and at Apple, he slammed on the brakes to rethink things.
There was something about the design that lacked purity, he felt.
“Why have this flat display if you’re going to glom all this stuff on its back?”
he asked Ive.
“We should let each element be true to itself.”
Jobs went home early that day to mull over the problem, then called Ive to come by.
